stem: Find the area of the region enclosed between the parabola \( y = x^{2} \) and the line \( y = [[m]]x + ([[c]]) \).
solution: The curves meet where \( x^{2} = [[m]]x + ([[c]]) \), namely at \( x = [[r1]] \) and \( x = [[r2]] \). Between these the line lies above the parabola, so the area is \( \int_{[[r1]]}^{[[r2]]}\left([[m]]x + ([[c]]) - x^{2}\right)dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This doubles the correct area, as if the antiderivative factor of \( \tfrac16 \) were \( \tfrac13 \).
This triples the correct area (using \( \tfrac12 \) instead of \( \tfrac16 \)).
This is the square of the horizontal width, not the enclosed area.
This integrates \( \text{bottom} - \text{top} \), giving the negative of the area.
This is off by one from the correct area.

stem: The curve \( y = x(x-[[p]])(x-[[q]]) \) crosses the \( x \)-axis at \( x = 0, [[p]], [[q]] \), lying above the axis on part of \( [0,[[q]]] \) and below it on the rest. Find the TOTAL area of the region(s) enclosed between the curve and the \( x \)-axis on \( 0 \le x \le [[q]] \).
solution: The curve is positive on \( (0,[[p]]) \) and negative on \( ([[p]],[[q]]) \), so the total (unsigned) area requires \( \int_{0}^{[[q]]}\left|x(x-[[p]])(x-[[q]])\right|dx = [[answer]] \), not the plain (signed) integral.
distractor_rationales: This is the plain signed integral \( \int_{0}^{[[q]]} x(x-[[p]])(x-[[q]])\,dx \), which cancels the two regions instead of adding their areas.
This only counts one of the two enclosed regions.
This double-counts the enclosed area.
This is off by \( [[p]] \) from the correct total area.
This is off by \( [[p]] \) from the correct total area.
This treats the region as a triangle-like shape with no calculus basis.

stem: Find the area of the region enclosed between the upward-opening parabola \( y = x^{2} + ([[p]]) \) and the downward-opening parabola \( y = -x^{2} + [[q]] \).
solution: The parabolas meet where \( x^{2}+([[p]]) = -x^{2}+[[q]] \), i.e. at \( x = \pm[[s]] \). The downward parabola lies above the upward one between these points, so the area is \( \int_{-[[s]]}^{[[s]]}\left((-x^{2}+[[q]]) - (x^{2}+([[p]]))\right)dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This doubles the correct area.
This halves the correct area.
This is the square of the horizontal width, not the enclosed area.
This integrates \( \text{bottom} - \text{top} \), giving the negative of the area.
This is off by one from the correct area.

stem: Find the area of the triangular region enclosed between the V-shaped curve \( y = |x - [[h]]| \) and the horizontal line \( y = [[k]] \).
solution: The curves meet where \( |x-[[h]]| = [[k]] \), i.e. \( x = [[h]]-[[k]] \) and \( x = [[h]]+[[k]] \). The line lies above the V-shape between these points, giving a triangle of base \( 2[[k]] \) and height \( [[k]] \): \( \int_{[[h]]-[[k]]}^{[[h]]+[[k]]}\left([[k]] - |x-[[h]]|\right)dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses the full base times height, \( 2k \cdot k \), instead of half that for a triangle.
This computes half the triangle's area as if the base were only \( k \).
This doubles the correct area.
This integrates \( \text{bottom} - \text{top} \), giving the negative of the area.
This is off by \( [[h]] \) from the correct area.

stem: On the interval \( [[a]] \le x \le [[b]] \), the curve \( y = x^{2} + [[p]] \) lies entirely above the line \( y = [[q]]x \). Find the area of the region between them on this interval.
solution: Since \( x^{2}+[[p]]-[[q]]x \) has no real roots (its discriminant \( [[q]]^{2}-4[[p]] \) is negative), the top curve stays above the line on the whole given interval, and the area is directly \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}\left((x^{2}+[[p]]) - [[q]]x\right)dx = [[answer]] \), with no need to solve for intersection points first.
distractor_rationales: This evaluates the vertical gap only at \( x=[[b]] \) minus at \( x=[[a]] \), instead of integrating it.
This uses the gap at \( x=[[b]] \) times the width, a right-rectangle approximation.
This integrates \( \text{bottom} - \text{top} \), giving the negative of the area.
This doubles the correct area.
This is off by \( [[p]] \) from the correct area.
